title: UAT-3: Auth injection not tested — verify OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD works end-to-end

title: UAT-4: Full workflow regression test for phase 6

close_reason: UAT passed in fresh session after Promise-lock fix. UAT-3 skipped (no password configured, auth header code verified in unit tests).

title: Design decision: autoStartAttempted once-per-lifetime prevents re-spawn after crash
description: D-06 intentionally prevents double-spawn loops, but means if OpenCode dies after initial auto-start, tools silently fail until MCP server restarts. Options: (1) keep as-is and document, (2) reset flag on health-check failure, (3) check health before returning early. File as design review item.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced autoStartAttempted boolean with Promise-based lock. startPromise resets to null after each spawn, enabling crash recovery. Concurrent dedup preserved.

title: v4: Multi-server registry (prefect add-server / list-servers / remove-server)
description:
Add a named-server registry so Claude Code can route tool calls to different OpenCode instances (e.g. different models or projects) without editing environment variables.

## What needs to be done

- CLI: prefect add-server <name> <host> <port> <model>, prefect remove-server <name>, prefect list-servers
- Registry stored at ~/.config/prefect/servers.json; read on every invocation (no in-process cache)
- All composite + session tools accept optional server: string param — routes to named server, defaults to first registered or OPENCODE_URL if registry is empty
- ensureOpencodeRunning() becomes server-aware — auto-starts the right instance for the targeted server
- CLAUDE.md gets a server registry section so Claude Code can make informed routing decisions without inspecting config files
- prefect init prompts for first server registration during setup

## Requirements
MULTI-01 through MULTI-08 (see .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md Future Requirements section)

value: Phase 11 UAT APPROVED 2026-04-30. All steps passed. HEAD: 87b6913 on master. Final findings: (1) messageID must start with 'msg' prefix — UUID rejected. (2) providerID must match configured server provider. (3) Guard fires correctly when AGENTS.md exists (no force). (4) force:true bypasses guard confirmed. (5) Model-driven: no write on empty/sparse dirs even with force — expected behavior. Phase 11 complete.

Phase 5 (Directory Infrastructure) design decisions: (1) Uniform directory schema on all 18 tools — only pass to SDK where endpoint accepts it, document which tools honor it. Consistent tool surface > inconsistent schema. (2) resolveDirectory() ends at undefined NOT process.cwd() — only send directory when explicitly provided via per-tool param or OPENCODE_DEFAULT_PROJECT. Sending process.cwd() unconditionally silently overrides OpenCode session-level directory tracking.
